message:
fix(db): enforce identity FTS cleanup on pool startup for all code paths

This commit addresses a critical regression where legacy identity FTS
artifacts (fts_agents, fts_projects, and their triggers) could survive
pool startup and cause rowid/index mismatch failures after crash/restart.

Changes:
- DbPool::new() now ALWAYS runs enforce_base_mode_cleanup() after
  init/migrations, regardless of whether migrations were applied
- Simplified cleanup flow by running cleanup on the same migration
  connection before dropping it, rather than opening a separate connection
- Updated pool_startup_strips_identity_fts_artifacts test to verify that
  identity FTS artifacts are removed (not preserved) after startup
- Added new test startup_cleanup_strips_identity_fts_when_migrations_disabled
  to cover the run_migrations=false startup path
- Extracted create_fixture_with_identity_fts() helper for test reuse

The identity FTS tables/triggers were added historically but are no
longer needed (message FTS is preserved for search functionality). When
present, they can cause corruption issues due to rowid mismatches between
the virtual tables and their backing content tables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
